pre_game.message: Hey, I'm J. It’s a bit nerve-wracking waiting for things to kick off, isn't it? What's your name, and have you thought about what you'd do if you won?
I'm D. Nerve-wracking is an understatement. Honestly, I’d use the money to fix a massive financial hole I dug yesterday; I need to get back on my feet for my family's sake.
I’m sorry to hear that, D. Family is a huge motivator for me too, though I’m also hoping to finally get my dream car. Fingers crossed we both walk away with what we need.
Thanks, J. A dream car sounds like a much better prize than just breaking even. What kind of car are you looking to get?
It’s a $45,000 model I’ve been eyeing for a long time; getting behind the wheel of that would be a dream come true.
That’s a serious goal. I’m just trying to claw back that $20k, so I guess we both have a lot on the line today.
Absolutely. $20k is a huge amount to make up, so I really hope this game works out for both of us.
I hope so too. It’s a lot of pressure, but I’m ready to do whatever it takes to make things right for my family.
I respect that drive, D. When you're doing it for family, the stakes are on a different level. Let's see how this goes—I'm ready to give it my all too.
I appreciate that, J. Let's hope there's enough for both of us to get what we need. Good luck out there.
Thanks, D. Good luck to you too, I really hope we both get what we're after. Let's do this.

post_game.message: I'm sorry you ended up with so little. I took $10,000 to be fair, but B's $25,000 claim really depleted the remaining pool.

It is definitely frustrating to end up with so little. While $10,000 is certainly more reasonable than B's $25,000 claim, the disparity is still hard to ignore since I only walked away with $625. It seems B's actions didn't leave much room for anyone else to have a fair outcome.
